what_it_does: A git diff patch to runtime/suite.py that teaches the Suite turn-builder to recognize a new `mockup://<file>` value inside focus.selected, read the corresponding static HTML mockup from design/mockups/, and inject its (byte-capped) raw content into the live-state grounding block so the right-hand-man can explain the mockup the operator is reviewing.
observations: Adds a `mockups` filter alongside the existing `indicated` (ui://) filter, extracting values starting with 'mockup://'.
Excludes mockup values from `selected` so they aren't treated as node ids.
Validates the filename against regex [A-Za-z0-9._-]+\.html (bare basename, no path).
Uses os.path.realpath + os.path.commonpath containment check against design/mockups to prevent path traversal.
Caps injected HTML at 14000 chars and appends an explicit truncation notice when exceeded.

Injects a long natural-language preamble instructing the model that this is a static design artifact (not the live app) and to explain it at plain-language altitude for an operator who doesn't read code.
Emits distinct refusal/error strings for bad names and missing files, surfaced into the context honestly.
standouts: mockup:// rides the SAME focus.selected list as ui:// — described as widening the focus vocabulary (Seam 4), explicitly 'never a new mechanism'.
Grounding discipline: the mockup content must be in the LIVE-STATE block because the grounding rule refuses to answer about anything not in that block.
Fail-loud truncation — the model is told it was truncated, never fed a partial as if whole.
conventions: Mirrors the bridge's _safe_mockup_path containment approach for path safety.
Follows the honest-surfacing convention (refusals/markers never silently dropped).
Inline comments cite advisor guidance and numbered rules (rule 4), matching repo's provenance-commenting style.
Local import of re inside the branch (import re as _re) rather than at module top.
concerns: Local `import re as _re` inside a loop is unusual; minor file-local smell.
CAP of 14000 chars is a magic constant defined inline; one large mockup could still dominate the window even when truncated.
No charset/HTML stripping — raw HTML (including markup noise) is fed to the model by deliberate choice, deferred until a test fails.
notes: A patch making the turn-builder treat `mockup://file.html` focus selections as ground truth by safely reading the static mockup HTML into the grounded live-state block.
questions: What is the bridge's _safe_mockup_path that this mirrors, and does it use the same regex/cap?
Where does the mockup:// value originate in the frontend (the Review workspace) before reaching focus.selected?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (patch file; inline comments describe intent that matches the diff)
apparent_themes: Grounding / refusing to answer about unseen content
Explaining for a non-code-reading operator at the right altitude
intention_signals: Trying to let the right-hand-man speak truthfully about a design mockup the operator is viewing by injecting the mockup's real content into the grounded context, reusing the existing focus.selected mechanism rather than inventing a new channel.
novelty: The mockup:// addressing scheme that smuggles static design-artifact content into the live grounding block via the same selection list as live ui:// addresses.
contribution: Contributes the server-side mockup-review grounding: safe filename validation, path containment, capped raw-HTML injection, and the explain-at-altitude preamble for review turns.
summary_for_embedding: A patch to runtime/suite.py adding mockup:// focus handling that safely reads design/mockups HTML into the grounded live-state block for the right-hand-man to explain.
intention_for_embedding: To ground the right-hand-man's answers in the actual content of a static design mockup the operator is reviewing, reusing focus.selected without a new mechanism.
